sentence: The _____ of the ancient manuscript required years of painstaking work.
hint: The noun form meaning the act of deciphering
explanation: DECIPHER → decipherment (suffix -ment: the scholarly process of decoding)

sentence: The philosopher's _____ on the nature of consciousness remains influential.
hint: A formal discussion or treatise
explanation: DISCOURSE → discourse (zero derivation: the word serves as both verb and noun in scholarly contexts)

sentence: The _____ implications of the new technology are still being debated.
hint: The adjective form relating to ethics
explanation: ETHICS → ethical (suffix -al: relating to or involving ethics)

sentence: The author's _____ style made the novel difficult to categorize.
hint: An adjective meaning mysterious or puzzling
explanation: ENIGMA → enigmatic (suffix -atic: having the quality of an enigma)

sentence: The _____ of power in the region led to decades of conflict.
hint: The noun form meaning the process of moving power away from the centre
explanation: CENTRE → decentralisation (prefix de- + suffix -isation: the process of distributing from a central authority)

sentence: The critic described the painting as deliberately _____.
hint: An adjective meaning intended to provoke
explanation: PROVOKE → provocative (suffix -ative: tending to provoke a reaction)

sentence: The _____ of the feudal system transformed European society.
hint: The noun form meaning the act of abolishing
explanation: ABOLISH → abolition (suffix -tion: the formal ending of a system or practice)

sentence: The researcher's _____ approach yielded unprecedented results.
hint: An adjective relating to methodology
explanation: METHOD → methodological (suffix -ological: relating to the systematic methods used)

sentence: The _____ of the two philosophical traditions produced a new school of thought.
hint: The noun form meaning the combination of ideas
explanation: SYNTHESISE → synthesis (noun form: the combining of separate elements into a whole)

sentence: The treaty was considered _____ by several member states.
hint: Not in accordance with the constitution
explanation: CONSTITUTION → unconstitutional (prefix un- + suffix -al: not conforming to the constitution)

sentence: The _____ of the poet's later works reveals a profound shift in worldview.
hint: The noun form meaning a detailed inspection
explanation: EXAMINE → examination (suffix -ation: the act of examining in detail)

sentence: The historian argued that the revolution was _____.
hint: Not able to be avoided
explanation: AVOID → unavoidable (prefix un- + suffix -able: impossible to prevent)

sentence: The _____ of the ancient civilization remains one of archaeology's greatest mysteries.
hint: The noun form of disappear
explanation: DISAPPEAR → disappearance (suffix -ance: the act of vanishing)

sentence: The scholar's _____ of the text challenged centuries of accepted interpretation.
hint: The noun form meaning interpreting again
explanation: REINTERPRET → reinterpretation (prefix re- + suffix -ation: a new interpretation)

sentence: The _____ nature of the evidence made definitive conclusions impossible.
hint: An adjective meaning open to more than one interpretation
explanation: AMBIGUITY → ambiguous (suffix -ous: having multiple possible meanings)

sentence: The _____ of the monarch's authority was a gradual process.
hint: The noun form meaning the removal of legitimacy
explanation: LEGITIMATE → delegitimisation (prefix de- + suffix -isation: the process of undermining legitimacy)

sentence: The professor's lecture on _____ linguistics was remarkably accessible.
hint: An adjective relating to computation
explanation: COMPUTE → computational (suffix -ational: relating to the use of computers or calculation)

sentence: The _____ of the artist's technique can be traced to Renaissance masters.
hint: The noun form meaning the process of improving
explanation: REFINE → refinement (suffix -ment: the process of making something more elegant or precise)

sentence: The committee found the proposal to be fundamentally _____.
hint: Not able to be made to work
explanation: WORK → unworkable (prefix un- + suffix -able: not capable of being implemented)

sentence: The _____ of the indigenous language was a tragic consequence of colonialism.
hint: The noun form meaning the dying out of something
explanation: EXTINCT → extinction (suffix -tion: the state of no longer existing)

sentence: The diplomat's _____ remarks nearly caused an international incident.
hint: Not circumspect — lacking caution
explanation: CIRCUMSPECT → incircumspect (prefix in-: not cautious or prudent)

sentence: The _____ between the empirical data and the theoretical model was troubling.
hint: The noun form meaning lack of agreement
explanation: CONGRUENT → incongruence (prefix in- + suffix -ence: the state of not being in agreement)

sentence: The _____ of the classical text brought it to a new generation of readers.
hint: The noun form meaning making something accessible to the general public
explanation: POPULAR → popularisation (suffix -isation: the process of making widely accessible)

sentence: The philosopher questioned the _____ of absolute moral truths.
hint: The noun form meaning the quality of being defensible
explanation: DEFEND → defensibility (suffix -ibility: the quality of being capable of being defended)

sentence: The _____ of the archaeological site yielded remarkable artefacts.
hint: The noun form meaning the process of digging out
explanation: EXCAVATE → excavation (suffix -tion: the systematic process of uncovering)

sentence: The literary critic noted the _____ quality of the author's prose.
hint: An adjective meaning surpassing ordinary limits
explanation: TRANSCEND → transcendent (suffix -ent: going beyond normal boundaries)
